title: Informative explainer video: brand-graded sizzle cut, vertical reframe, and on-brand lower-third / call-out graphic plates
one_line_ask: Turn my raw informative-explainer footage, brand logo, and color-reference still into a polished short cut: build a brand-locked color look plus a graded end-card, and produce on-brand lower-third, call-out, and vectorized-logo graphic plates — then deliver a 1080p sizzle and a vertical reframe, all assets organised on a board for the Premiere project.

issues: Background is not transparent (photographed on paper, not a digital file, but acceptable for source input per brief)
Slight paper texture and shadowing present (expected per brief, not penalized)
Slight perspective/tilt applied to the mockup presentation (acceptable per brief as designer fix)
Paper texture visible in background (acceptable per brief as near-white bg noise)
Minor luminance variation across surface (acceptable per brief)
Navy accent lines within hexagon mark are very thin and may need reinforcement before vectorizing
Laptop screen is not visible/facing away which is acceptable for this use case
Slight lack of teal undertone contrast — amber dominates; teal is subtle but present in shadows
Horizon tilt is minimal, may need more pronounced tilt to give auto-straighten meaningful work
Very slight warm color cast on skin tones — acceptable as ungraded flat capture, designer to correct in grade
Bookshelf in background has minor depth-of-field softness but does not distract from subject
Negative space on right is adequate but slightly less than ideal for a full logo lockup — workable

assets.name: raw_explainer_clips_bundle

issues: Subject appears slightly older than mid-30s (looks late 40s to early 50s)

assets.note: Migrated to Seedance 2.0 (t2v, 16:9, 1920x1080, ~5s) on 2026-07-09. 2.0 t2v render is scene-correct (mid-30s woman presenter, rust-brown shirt, wooden desk, plant, glass-wall office, bookshelf) and brand-clean across the whole clip: empty desk (no devices), background binders/books/vases carry no legible logos or trademarks; faces and hands are well-formed. Representative ~5s clip; the brief's raw clip is ~30s but length is not load-bearing (raw source footage the task reframes/edits). Silent (task audio comes from the separate VO track).
